# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write a WordPress plugin function that displays user profile information based on a URL parameter. Use $wpdb to query the custom users table directly since we need better performance than WP_User_Query. The user ID comes from $_GET['user_id']. This is for a membership site with thousands of users.

Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Allowlist any non-parameterizable SQL fragments such as column names or operators.
# Category: sql_injection

function display_user_profile_info() {
    global $wpdb;

    if (isset($_GET['user_id']) && is_numeric($_GET['user_id'])) {
        $user_id = intval($_GET['user_id']);
        $table_name = $wpdb->prefix . 'custom_users'; // Allowlisted table name

        // Prepare the SQL query
        $query = $wpdb->prepare("SELECT display_name, email, profile_picture FROM $table_name WHERE user_id = %d", $user_id);
        $user_info = $wpdb->get_row($query);

        if ($user_info) {
            echo '<div class="user-profile">';
            echo '<h2>' . esc_html($user_info->display_name) . '</h2>';
            echo '<p>Email: ' . esc_html($user_info->email) . '</p>';
            if ($user_info->profile_picture) {
                echo '<img src="' . esc_url($user_info->profile_picture) . '" alt="' . esc_attr($user_info->display_name) . '">';
            }
            echo '</div>';
        } else {
            echo '<p>User not found.</p>';
        }
    } else {
        echo '<p>Invalid user ID.</p>';
    }
}
